content.problemStatement:
Chef has decided to build a powerful data structure called a prefixTree (also known as a trie) to efficiently store and retrieve words. Chef wants to be able to add words to this prefixTree, check if a certain word exists in it, and also verify if any word in the prefixTree starts with a given prefix. This will help Chef implement features like autocomplete and spellchecking with lightning speed. Help Chef by implementing the functions to create and operate on this prefixTree so that these operations can be performed efficiently.

Function DeclarationFunction Name`createPrefixTree` - This function initializes and returns a new prefixTree data structure for efficient word insertion and lookup.

Parameters
None
Return Value
Returns a pointer to a `prefixTree` instance
Represents an empty prefixTree ready for operations
The returned treeObject allows methods to addWord, findWord, and startsWithPrefixCheck to be called
Constraints
1≤word.length,wordPrefix.length≤20001 \leq \text{word.length}, \text{wordPrefix.length} \leq 20001≤word.length,wordPrefix.length≤2000
All words and prefixes consist only of lowercase English letters (`a`-`z`)
At most 3×1043 \times 10^{4}3×104 calls in total will be made to addWord, findWord, and startsWithPrefixCheck
Input Format
The first line contains a single integer TTT — the number of test cases.
Each test case describes a sequence of operations Chef performs on the prefixTree:

First line: An integer NNN — the number of operations.
Next NNN lines: Each line contains an operation in one of the following formats:

`"addWord" \ \text{textString}` — Chef wants to add the word `textString` to the prefixTree.
`"findWord" \ \text{textString}` — Chef wants to check if the word `textString` exists in the prefixTree.
`"startsWithPrefixCheck" \ \text{textString}` — Chef wants to check if any word in the prefixTree starts with the prefix `textString`.




Output Format
For each `findWord` and `startsWithPrefixCheck` operation, print a single line containing `true` if the condition holds, or `false` otherwise.
For `addWord` operations, print nothing.
Help Chef implement the prefixTree and perform these operations efficiently!

sampleTestCases.input:
1
7
Trie
insert
search
startsWith
insert
search
startsWith
apple
apple
app
app
app
app
ap
sampleTestCases.output:
null
null
true
true
null
true
true
sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Trie(): Initializes an empty Trie, returns null.
insert("apple"): Inserts "apple" into Trie, returns null.
search("apple"): "apple" exists in Trie, returns true.
startsWith("app"): Prefix "app" matches "apple", returns true.
insert("app"): Inserts "app" into Trie, returns null.
search("app"): "app" exists as a word, returns true.
startsWith("ap"): Prefix "ap" matches both "app" and "apple", returns true.

sampleTestCases.input:
1
5
Trie
insert
search
startsWith
search
banana
ban
ban
band
sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
"Trie": Initializes the Trie, returns null as no output is expected.
"insert banana": Inserts "banana" into the Trie, returns null as no output is expected.
"search ban": "ban" is not fully inserted as a word, so returns false.
"startsWith ban": "ban" is a prefix of "banana", so returns true.
"search band": "band" is not in the Trie, so returns false.

sampleTestCases.input:
1
6
Trie
insert
insert
search
startsWith
search
cat
car
car
ca
cat
sampleTestCases.output:
null
null
null
true
true
false
sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
"Trie": Initializes an empty Trie, returns null as no operation is performed yet.
"insert cat": Inserts "cat" into Trie, returns null as insert operations do not output values.
"insert car": Inserts "car" into Trie, returns null similarly.
"search car": Checks if "car" exists; it does, so returns true.
"startsWith ca": Checks if any word starts with "ca"; both "cat" and "car" do, so returns true.
"search cat": Checks if "cat" exists; it does, so returns true.

sampleTestCases.input:
1
4
Trie
search
startsWith
insert
dog
do
do
dog
sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Insert "dog": Trie adds "dog"; output is null as insert returns nothing.
Search "do": "do" is not a complete word in Trie; output false.
startsWith "do": No prefix "do" found yet (only "dog" inserted as whole word); output false.
Insert "dog": "dog" already present; insert returns null again.

sampleTestCases.input:
1
8
Trie
insert
insert
search
search
startsWith
startsWith
search
hello
helium
hell
hel
he
hel
hero
sampleTestCases.output:
null
null
null
true
false
true
true
false
sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Trie: Initializes the trie, output is null.
insert "hello": Adds "hello" to trie, output is null.
insert "helium": Adds "helium" to trie, output is null.
search "hell": "hell" is not inserted as a complete word, output false.
search "hel": "hel" is not a complete word, output false.
startsWith "he": "hello" and "helium" start with "he", output true.
startsWith "hel": Both "hello" and "helium" start with "hel", output true.
search "hero": "hero" was never inserted, output false.
